summary: Syntax check and module imports pass cleanly. mypy strict mode produces 3 minor `unused-ignore` warnings (no blockers). ruff reports 454 findings across the tree — the vast majority (SLF001 x180, ANN401 x102, PLR2004 x50, INP001 x23) are stylistic noise driven by an intentionally strict `extend-select = ["ALL"]` config in pyproject.toml, and against tests/ that only excludes S101/D/ARG. Source-level findings: 88 ruff + 3 mypy, all minor, no real defects. Anti-pattern checks (getattr/hasattr/string-patch/bare-type-ignore/unittest.TestCase) pass with only legitimate interface-introspection uses in tests.
tools_run: anti-pattern grep (hasattr/getattr, @patch string form, bare type:ignore, pip install, unittest.TestCase)

findings.summary: Unused `# type: ignore[attr-defined]` on the `add_mcp_server` re-export.
findings.detail: mypy reports the suppression as `unused-ignore`. The `TinyAgent.add_mcp_server` attribute is now visible at module scope so the suppression is no longer needed. Remove the comment to keep `warn_unused_ignores = true` clean.

findings.summary: Unused `# type: ignore[attr-defined]` on the `mcp.types.Tool` runtime import fallback.
findings.detail: mcp is installed in pyproject.toml; the `try/except` no longer needs the suppression. Remove to keep CI clean.

findings.summary: Unused `# type: ignore[no-redef,misc]` on the `MCPTool` fallback class.
findings.detail: The fallback class does not actually trigger either rule under the installed mcp. Remove the suppression.

findings.summary: Import block is un-sorted (I001 fixable with ruff --fix).
findings.detail: The stdlib import block is reorderable. Single-line `ruff check --fix tinyagent.py` resolves.

findings.summary: Unused `# noqa: F401` directive on `import asyncio` (RUF100).
findings.detail: asyncio is used at runtime, so F401 never fires — the noqa from the stub-era imports is dead. Bulk-clean: remove all six RUF100 noqa comments on lines 34/35/37/41/43/48.
Module comment says it is used at type hints, but no actual usage is present.
`pydantic` namespace is imported but only `BaseModel`, `ConfigDict`, `Field` are referenced via the `from pydantic import ...` line.
Not referenced anywhere in tinyagent.py — was imported defensively.
Mentioned by name only in the TYPE_CHECKING block; never referenced.
Plan commentary on import layout. Not executed. Informational.
Two `__all__` declarations in the module (line 92 and line 2689); ruff expects sorted strings. Cosmetic.

findings.summary: Exception class `AgentCancel` violates N818 (should end with `Error`).
findings.detail: Deliberate per plan §2 section 6 — `AgentCancel` is part of the public API surface and is not an error class. Add `N818` to the per-file ignores for the section, or document the carve-out in pyproject.toml.

findings.summary: Use of `assert` detected in production code (S101).
findings.detail: Located inside `CallbackRegistry.dispatch_sync`. Tests do not exercise the `assert` branch but it is defensible as a precondition guard; consider downgrading to a typed raise, or ignore S101 in non-test paths.
Cosmetic; standard library, no side effects, lazy-load is intentional to keep tool-specific imports near use. Move to module top if lint-cleanliness matters.
Likely a deferred runtime import inside `MCPServer.connect` or similar. Confirm the deferred semantics and either leave with a `noqa` or hoist.
Per project style: assign the message to a local first. Two additional EM102 sites at lines 1318/2171/2184/2192 — same cosmetic fix.
Single source file line-length violation. Wrap or reformat.
Inside the pricing cost-estimation function. Could collapse branches or extract helpers. Cosmetic relative to the strict ruff config.

findings.summary: Async function declared with a `timeout` parameter (ASYNC109).
findings.detail: This is the canonical `http_get` tool signature with a timeout parameter — the rule wants `AnyIO`/`asyncio.timeout`. Informational; intentionally exposed for tool consumers.
Cosmetic; sort the tuple alphabetically.
Python 3.11+ moved these to `collections.abc`; `typing.AsyncIterator` is deprecated in 3.12+. Cosmetic but forward-compatible cleanup.
Plan §2 section 11 says the return-type is `MCPServer`; confirm `__aenter__` matches `self`. May be a TypedDict / type-alias mismatch.

findings.summary: Many `Any` annotations on Context fields (ANN401 x10 in production code).
findings.detail: The `Context = SimpleNamespace(...)` design uses dynamic typing by intention (per plan §2 section 8). Cosmetic under strict ruff; consider adopting `TypedDict`/dataclass or adding `# noqa: ANN401` annotations.
Required by the design (sync wrapper must pin the worker-thread loop on the registry). Two SLF001 sites in `tinyagent.py`: 2628 and 2635. Add `# noqa: SLF001` or accept the noise.

findings.summary: Branch/return count exceeds ruff PLR0911/PLR0912 limits.
findings.detail: Pricing function is the longest per-file function. Could be split into `pricing_fn(pricing_fn_callable, pricing, model_str)` + `pricing_table(pricing, model_str)` helpers.

findings.summary: Import block un-sorted plus F401/PLR0402/EM102/SIM105 (5 findings).
findings.detail: Test fixture; ruff check passes `--fix` cleanly. Five auto-fixable sites in one file.

findings.summary: `from tinyagent import ...` inside a function (PLC0415 x2).
findings.detail: Lazy module import to avoid a heavy-import cycle if `tinyagent` is already loaded. Acceptable but inconsistent with PEP 8.
Cosmetic; replace `–` with `-`.

findings.summary: No newline at end of file (W292).
findings.detail: Single newline missing. Trivial fix.

findings.summary: Local variable `agent_span` assigned but never used (F841).
findings.detail: Dead local in test body — likely a leftover from refactoring.
Plus 5 more E501 sites in test files: test_mcp_stdio.py:306, test_agent_loop.py:438, test_exceptions.py:40/140. Wrap or break.

78 PLR2004 hits across tests; the strict ruff rule conflicts with the project's style of asserting on concrete defaults. Either add PLR2004 to the per-file ignores for tests/ or replace literals with module constants.

findings.summary: Private-member access on `CallbackRegistry._hooks` / `_loop` (SLF001 x22 in test_callback_registry.py).
findings.detail: Tests deliberately introspect internal storage to validate the dict-backed storage model per plan §5 / §0 C5. Add SLF001 to the tests' per-file ignores.

findings.summary: `getattr(trace, '_TRACER_PROVIDER_SET_ONCE', None)` is intrinsic to the integration smoke test.
findings.detail: Single SLF-style access; acceptable in tests but contributes to the overall noise count.
Plan §2 says '__all__ re-affirmation' at the footer; ruff treats this as a duplicate unsorted list. Either drop the footer declaration or sort and consolidate.

findings.summary: No secrets detected in changed files.
findings.detail: Scanned for sk-/ghp_/AKIA tokens and hard-coded IP patterns; none found.

No bare `type: ignore` (must be `type: ignore[code]`), no string-based `@patch(...)` decorators, no `unittest.TestCase` classes, no functional `pip install` in code (only in test docstrings/comments).

findings.summary: Tests carry 357 ruff findings dominated by SLF001 (x180) and ANN401 (x102).
findings.detail: Per the strict `extend-select = ["ALL"]` config; tests would benefit from additional per-file-ignores (ANN, SLF, PLR, INP, SIM, FBT, ASYNC, ERA, PT) to align ruff with the test-helper conventions used in code comments (`# narrow via cast — no getattr/hasattr`).
Module imports under `python3` without warnings; `tinyagent.calculate` and `tinyagent.http_get` resolve.
